title: Is First Trust's FTGC Commodities ETF a Buy After Climber Capital Purchased Shares Worth $3.8 Million?
author: Robert Izquierdo
description: Swiss wealth management firm Climber Capital SA initiated a new $3.76 million position in First Trust's Global Tactical Commodity Strategy Fund (FTGC), acquiring 145,633 shares. The ETF, which has delivered a 46.2% one-year return and offers a 14.8% dividend yield, provides diversified commodity exposure through active management. While the purchase signals bullish sentiment, investors should consider the fund's volatility and 0.98% expense ratio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: New institutional investment from Climber Capital signals confidence in the fund. Strong performance metrics (46.2% one-year return, 14.8% dividend yield) and substantial AUM ($2.7 billion) support bullish outlook, though high expense ratio and commodity volatility present offsetting concerns.

title: Arvest Adds $3.1 Million Position in FTGC ETF
author: Pamela Kock
description: Arvest Investments purchased 119,876 additional shares of First Trust Global Tactical Commodity Strategy Fund (FTGC) in Q1 2026, valued at $3.1 million. The position now represents 1.47% of Arvest's assets under management. FTGC is an actively managed ETF targeting diversified global commodities with a 15.37% trailing dividend yield and has outperformed the S&P 500 by 12.82 percentage points over the past year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: FTGC demonstrates strong performance with 41.19% one-year returns, outperforming the S&P 500 by 12.82 percentage points. The fund offers an attractive 15.37% trailing dividend yield and recently hit a 52-week high. Institutional investor interest, including purchases by other wealth managers, further supports positive sentiment.

title: Flaharty Sold $13 Million of First Trust Global Tactical Commodity Strategy Fund
author: John Ballard
description: Flaharty Asset Management reduced its position in First Trust Global Tactical Commodity Strategy Fund (FTGC) by selling 524,260 shares worth approximately $13.6 million in Q4 2025. The reduction reflects expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts, as commodities typically underperform in lower interest rate environments. The firm also trimmed positions in other commodity and fixed-income ETFs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Significant position reduction by major investor signals reduced confidence in commodity exposure amid anticipated rate cuts. The sale of approximately half the stake indicates expectation of underperformance in a lower rate environment.
Flaharty reduced its position in this fixed-income ETF as part of portfolio adjustments ahead of anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts.
Position trimmed by Flaharty as part of broader reduction in fixed-income holdings in anticipation of changing interest rate environment.
Energy sector position reduced by Flaharty, consistent with de-risking from commodity-related investments ahead of expected rate cuts.

title: Climber Capital Sells $3.5 Million of First Trust Global Tactical Commodity Strategy ETF
author: John Ballard
description: Climber Capital SA completely exited its $3.46 million position in First Trust Global Tactical Commodity Strategy Fund (FTGC), selling 144,878 shares. The liquidation reflects weakness in commodity prices for oil and agricultural products. The fund manager reallocated proceeds into stocks and bonds, betting on improved economic outlook with stronger manufacturing activity and lower interest rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Complete liquidation by Climber Capital indicates loss of confidence in commodity exposure due to recent weakness in oil and agricultural prices. The fund underperformed S&P 500 by 1.46 percentage points over one year.
Climber Capital increased allocation to this index fund, reflecting optimism about broader stock market expansion beyond tech sector as manufacturing strengthens and economic outlook improves.
Increased allocation reflects expectations of further Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026, which would benefit bond funds.
Increased allocation as part of bond fund strategy, benefiting from anticipated interest rate cuts.
Increased allocation reflects expectations that lower interest rates will benefit dividend-paying stocks.
